channel.title: Newest Python PEPs
channel.description: Newest Python Enhancement Proposals (PEPs): Information on new language features and some meta-information like release procedure and schedules.

item.title: PEP 759: External Wheel Hosting
item.description: This PEP proposes a mechanism by which projects hosted on pypi.org can safely host wheel artifacts on external sites other than PyPI. This PEP explicitly does not propose external hosting of projects, packages, or their metadata. That functionality is already available by externally hosting independent package indexes. Because this PEP only provides a mechanism for projects to customize the download URL for specific released wheel artifacts, dependency resolution as already implemented by common installer tools such as pip and uv does not need to change.
item.author: Barry Warsaw (barry@python.org), Ethan Smith (ethan@ethanhs.me)

item.title: PEP 758: Allow ``except`` and ``except*`` expressions without parentheses
item.description: This PEP 1 proposes to allow unparenthesized except and except* blocks in Python’s exception handling syntax. Currently, when catching multiple exceptions, parentheses are required around the exception types. This was a Python 2 remnant. This PEP suggests allowing the omission of these parentheses, simplifying the syntax, making it more consistent with other parts of the syntax that make parentheses optional, and improving readability in certain cases.
item.author: Pablo Galindo (pablogsal@python.org), Brett Cannon (brett@python.org)

item.title: PEP 757: C API to import-export Python integers
item.description: Add a new C API to import and export Python integers, int objects: especially PyLongWriter_Create() and PyLong_Export() functions.
item.author: Sergey B Kirpichev (skirpichev@gmail.com), Victor Stinner (vstinner@python.org)

item.title: PEP 756: Add PyUnicode_Export() and PyUnicode_Import() C functions
item.description: Add functions to the limited C API version 3.14:
item.author: Victor Stinner (vstinner@python.org)

item.title: PEP 755: Implicit namespace policy for PyPI
item.description: This PEP codifies an implementation of PEP 752 for PyPI 1.
item.author: Ofek Lev (ofekmeister@gmail.com)

item.title: PEP 753: Uniform project URLs in core metadata
item.description: This PEP recommends two discrete changes to the processing of core metadata by indices (such as PyPI) and other core metadata consumers:
item.author: William Woodruff (william@yossarian.net), Facundo Tuesca (facundo.tuesca@trailofbits.com)

item.title: PEP 752: Implicit namespaces for package repositories
item.description: This PEP specifies a way for organizations to reserve package name prefixes for future uploads.
item.author: Ofek Lev (ofekmeister@gmail.com)

item.title: PEP 751: A file format to list Python dependencies for installation reproducibility
item.description: This PEP proposes a new file format for dependency specification to enable reproducible installation in a Python environment. The format is designed to be human-readable and machine-generated. Installers consuming the file should be able to evaluate each package in question in isolation, with no need for dependency resolution at install-time.
item.author: Brett Cannon (brett@python.org)

item.title: PEP 750: Tag Strings For Writing Domain-Specific Languages
item.description: This PEP introduces tag strings for custom, repeatable string processing. Tag strings are an extension to f-strings, with a custom function – the “tag” – in place of the f prefix. This function can then provide rich features such as safety checks, lazy evaluation, domain-specific languages (DSLs) for web templating, and more.
item.author: Jim Baker (jim.baker@python.org), Guido van Rossum (guido@python.org), Paul Everitt (pauleveritt@me.com)

item.title: PEP 2026: Calendar versioning for Python
item.description: This PEP proposes updating the versioning scheme for Python to include the calendar year.
item.author: Hugo van Kemenade
